title: verbascum carmanicum subsp. kermanense
intro: Verbascum carmanicum subsp. kermanense, commonly known as Kerman Mullein, is a species of flowering plant in the figwort family that is native to Iran and the Middle East. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Kerman Mullein include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Kerman Mullein can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to create salves and creams for topical application.
